description: §19.6.6 — E-ERROR-005 fires on PARTIAL coverage. The enum has two reachable error variants: NotFound (HAS a `renders` clause) and Timeout (has NONE). The `<errorBoundary>` declares NO `fallback`. NotFound is displayable via its own `renders`, but Timeout has neither a `renders` clause nor a boundary `fallback`, so the boundary cannot display it → E-ERROR-005. This proves the exhaustiveness check is PER-VARIANT (every reachable variant must be covered), not satisfied by any single covered variant. Sharpens the `variant-renders` clean case, which pairs `renders` WITH a `fallback` so the second variant is covered.
rationale: §19.6.6: EVERY error variant reachable inside a boundary SHALL be covered by a `renders` clause OR the boundary's `fallback`. NotFound is covered by its `renders`; Timeout is covered by neither, so the per-variant check fails on Timeout → E-ERROR-005. E-ERROR-002 stays silent (the call is inside a boundary, §19.6.6).
